title: pseudolysimachion spicatum subsp. klokovii
intro: Pseudolysimachion spicatum subsp. klokovii, also known as Klokov's Pseudolysimachion or Klokov's whorled wintergreen, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, native to Ukraine. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Pseudolysimachion spicatum subsp. klokovii include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and treating various gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and essential oil extracts, which are used to promote digestion, calm the nervous system, and alleviate respiratory problems.
